# Questions in Livestream 259

Live Stream #259: A Turn of the Seasons

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=951" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:51</a> | What causes Vancouver's purple street lamps? | He says the purple color comes from defective LED street lamps. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=1428" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:48</a> | Could someone ask God to annihilate them after final judgment instead of going to hell? | He says no. In his view that is not an available option because humans bear the image of God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=1614" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:54</a> | Would the image of God being present in hell profane it? | He says no, because the issue is human autonomy and the consequences of one's choice, not profanation of the divine image. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=1945" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:25</a> | What is worse: ham radio self-study books or online ancient Egyptian translators? | He says both are bad in different ways, but criticizes online Egyptian translators as especially unreliable because they depend on incompetent word lists. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=2403" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:03</a> | How bad has wokeism gotten in Canada? | He says it has become severe enough that human-rights tribunals can impose large fines or penalties outside ordinary criminal-law protections. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=2494" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:34</a> | Are Canadian human-rights tribunals subject to the Charter or normal constitutional protections? | He says not in the normal way, which is exactly why he considers them dangerous. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=2608" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:28</a> | Can defendants before Canadian human-rights tribunals be pardoned? | No. He says there is no pardon because tribunal findings are treated as social-rights violations rather than ordinary criminal convictions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=2639" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:59</a> | Is there a Canadian push to import a U.S.-style First Amendment to counter tribunal overreach? | He says Canada already has an analogue in the Charter, but without the same kind of absolute constitutional structure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=2779" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:19</a> | Can Parliament entrench a free-speech law into Canada's constitution to restrain tribunals? | He says constitutional amendment is harder than that and would still face Canada's existing exceptions and jurisprudence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=2842" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:22</a> | Before the Levitical priesthood, were the `priests` in Exodus 19 former pagan priests? | He says they were professional priests by trade, likely coming out of pagan priestly backgrounds, who were making the switch to Yahweh worship before the Aaronic priesthood was established. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=3075" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:15</a> | How can we know there were priests in Israel before the Aaronic priesthood? | He points to the Korah rebellion and related passages as evidence that prior priestly status was already assumed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=3761" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:41</a> | Is Matthew 10:37-42 the fullness of discipleship? | Yes. He says discipleship means disciplined following of Christ and loving Christ above every other relationship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=4335" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:15</a> | Is there a limit to plausible deniability under a Christian worldview? | Yes. He says the limit is negligence: if someone knew or should have known, plausible deniability no longer applies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=4398" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:18</a> | If Genesis ages are clan ages, why include Sarah's age at death when clans are mostly patrilineal? | He says clans are mostly patrilineal but not strictly so, and Sarah's age could mark her old age at childbirth or preserve a maternal clan line. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=6766" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:46</a> | Did Michael Heiser get the divine-council idea from Talmudic or Second Temple writings? | He says yes, especially from Second Temple literature, and argues that the idea is much later than the Bronze Age or early Iron Age. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=7266" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:06</a> | Is there anything right in Heiser's divine-council view? | He says Heiser is partly right about Second Temple Jewish belief, but wrong to project that theology back across all biblical history. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=8581" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:23:01</a> | Are you a ham radio operator? | Yes. He says he had just received his certificates and gives his call sign. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=8885" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:05</a> | Where do scholars get the idea that El distributed lands to other gods? | He says the main text behind that reading is Deuteronomy 32. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=9774" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:54</a> | Was Arius a Trinitarian? | No. He describes Arius as non-Trinitarian and specifically Arian in theology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=11840" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:20</a> | Are there commentaries or study Bibles that take the late date? | Some do. He says a few commentaries and study Bibles present late-date options, though most still favor the early date. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=12504" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:24</a> | How were the pyramids built? | He says the basic answer is boats, sledges, ropes, and other ordinary transport technology rather than a lost mystery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/v9NevZrXaIo?t=12904" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:35:04</a> | Is there a good book comparing old-earth creationism, theistic evolution, and young-earth creationism? | He advises starting with Scripture itself rather than jumping first into systems defending any one camp. |
